section: Chapter 12: Earth, Moon, and the Sun

questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
questions.correct_answer: Rotation of Earth on its axis
questions.explanation: The side facing the Sun experiences day, and the opposite side experiences night.

questions.question: What is Earth's revolution?
questions.correct_answer: Earth's orbit around the Sun once every 365.25 days
questions.explanation: Earth’s revolution, along with axial tilt, causes seasons.

questions.question: Why do we have seasons?
questions.correct_answer: Because Earth’s axis is tilted at 23.5° relative to its orbit around the Sun
questions.explanation: Tilt changes the angle and intensity of sunlight, leading to seasonal variations.

questions.question: What are the phases of the Moon?
questions.correct_answer: New Moon, crescent, first quarter, gibbous, and full Moon
questions.explanation: Moon phases are due to its position relative to Earth and Sun.

questions.question: When does a solar eclipse occur?
questions.correct_answer: When the Moon comes between Earth and the Sun
questions.explanation: This happens only during a new Moon and can be partial or total.

questions.question: When does a lunar eclipse occur?
questions.correct_answer: When Earth comes between the Sun and Moon
questions.explanation: Earth’s shadow falls on the Moon, happening only during a full Moon.

questions.question: What is a synodic month?
questions.correct_answer: The time taken for the Moon to complete a full cycle of phases (~29.5 days)
questions.explanation: It is measured from one new Moon to the next.

questions.question: Why do eclipses not occur every month?
questions.correct_answer: Because the Moon’s orbit is tilted about 5° to the ecliptic plane
questions.explanation: Most months, the Moon passes above or below the Sun-Earth line, preventing eclipses.

questions.question: Why are day and night lengths different at different latitudes?
questions.correct_answer: Because of Earth’s tilt and revolution around the Sun
questions.explanation: Polar regions can have continuous daylight or darkness, while equator has nearly equal day-night lengths.

questions.question: Do seasons depend on Earth’s distance from the Sun?
questions.correct_answer: No, seasons are caused by the tilt of Earth’s axis
questions.explanation: Even though Earth’s orbit is elliptical, tilt determines sunlight angle and temperature variations.

notes.title: Rotation of Earth
notes.content: Causes day and night; completes one rotation in 24 hours.

notes.title: Revolution of Earth
notes.content: Earth orbits the Sun in ~365.25 days; causes seasons.

notes.title: Tilt of Earth’s Axis
notes.content: Tilted at 23.5°, affects sunlight angle and causes seasonal variations.

notes.title: Moon Phases
notes.content: Changes from new Moon to full Moon over ~29.5 days due to relative positions of Sun, Earth, and Moon.

notes.title: Solar and Lunar Eclipses
notes.content: Solar: Moon blocks Sun (new Moon). Lunar: Earth blocks Sun’s light on Moon (full Moon).

facts.fact: Earth rotates once every 24 hours, causing day and night.
Earth revolves around the Sun in an elliptical orbit once every 365.25 days.
Moon phases cycle every 29.5 days (synodic month).
Solar eclipses occur only during new Moon; lunar eclipses occur only during full Moon.
Tilt of Earth’s axis, not distance from Sun, causes seasons.
